commands.description: Store Garmin credentials in the OS keyring. Interactive by default; operator-only (requires a live password).

flags.help: Read the Garmin password from a single line on stdin (for non-interactive use)
flags.name: --password-stdin

flags.help: Read the Garmin password from the named environment variable
flags.name: --password-env

commands.description: Store Intervals.icu credentials in the OS keyring. Interactive by default; operator-only (requires a live API key).

flags.help: Intervals.icu athlete id (prompts if omitted)
flags.name: --athlete-id

flags.help: Read the Intervals.icu API key from a single line on stdin (for non-interactive use)
flags.name: --api-key-stdin

flags.help: Read the Intervals.icu API key from the named environment variable
flags.name: --api-key-env

commands.description: Remove stored credentials from the OS keyring. Idempotent — removing absent credentials is a no-op. Env-var-supplied credentials are never touched.

flags.help: Which credential set to remove. 'all' removes both Garmin and Intervals.icu keyring entries. Env-var-supplied credentials are never touched.
flags.name: --source

commands.description: Report whether Garmin and Intervals.icu credentials are configured. Presence only — never emits the secret itself.
Versioned tarball of state.db + JSONL audit logs + manifest. Read-only on local state. See reporting/docs/recovery.md for the recovery contract.

flags.help: Output tarball path. Default: ./hai-backup-<UTC>.tar.gz
flags.name: --dest

flags.help: State DB path (default: $HAI_STATE_DB or platform default).
flags.name: --db-path

flags.help: Writeback/intake root (JSONL logs). Default: $HAI_BASE_DIR or ~/.health_agent.
flags.name: --base-dir

commands.description: Emit the agent-CLI-contract manifest describing every subcommand's mutation class, idempotency, JSON output, and exit codes. The authoritative surface the routing skill consumes.

flags.help: Render the manifest as the contract markdown doc on stdout instead of JSON. Used by the doc regenerator.
flags.name: --markdown

flags.help: Render a one-page user-readable overview grouped by workflow stage. Skips the schema-annotation columns; for the agent-facing manifest use --json (default).
flags.name: --human

flags.help: No-op alias: JSON is already the default emit. Provided so agents and docs can pass `--json` explicitly without argparse rejecting the flag.
flags.name: --json

commands.description: Normalize pulled evidence into CleanedEvidence + RawSummary JSON and project accepted state rows. Best-effort projection when --db-path is absent.

flags.help: Path to a JSON file produced by `hai pull`
flags.name: --evidence-json

flags.help: State DB path (default: $HAI_STATE_DB or platform default). If the DB is absent, projection is skipped with a stderr note; stdout is unchanged.
flags.name: --db-path

commands.description: Diff user thresholds.toml against DEFAULT_THRESHOLDS, leaf by leaf.

flags.help: Override source path.
flags.name: --path

commands.description: Scaffold a default thresholds.toml at the user-config path.

flags.help: Override destination path (default: platformdirs user_config_dir)
flags.name: --path

flags.help: Overwrite an existing thresholds.toml
flags.name: --force

commands.description: Print the effective merged threshold configuration (defaults + overrides).

flags.help: Override source path (default: platformdirs user_config_dir)
flags.name: --path

commands.description: Validate user thresholds.toml against DEFAULT_THRESHOLDS shape.

flags.help: Override source path.
flags.name: --path

flags.help: Treat unknown leaf keys as blocking errors.
flags.name: --strict

commands.description: Morning orchestrator: deterministic stages run end-to-end (pull → clean → snapshot → gaps → proposal_gate). The agent then invokes the 6 per-domain readiness skills, posts DomainProposal rows via `hai propose --domain <d>`, and re-runs `hai daily` to advance the gate to `complete` and trigger synthesis. `--domains <csv>` narrows the expected set for partial-day planning.

flags.help: Writeback/intake root. review_events.jsonl is appended here after synthesis. Default: $HAI_BASE_DIR or ~/.health_agent.
flags.name: --base-dir

flags.help: Civil date to orchestrate for, ISO-8601. Default: today UTC.
flags.name: --as-of

flags.help: User whose daily pipeline to orchestrate.
flags.name: --user-id

flags.help: State DB path (default: `$HAI_STATE_DB` or `~/.local/share/health_agent_infra/state.db`).
flags.name: --db-path

flags.help: Legacy flag: equivalent to --source garmin_live. Garmin Connect's login surface is rate-limited and unreliable for live scraping; prefer `--source intervals_icu` (the v0.1.6+ supported live source).
flags.name: --live

flags.help: Evidence source for the pull stage. Same semantics as `hai pull --source`. Default (v0.1.6+): intervals_icu when credentials are configured, else csv.
flags.name: --source

flags.help: Trailing window for live pull series (matches `hai pull --history-days`).
flags.name: --history-days

flags.help: Skip the pull + clean stages. Assumes state already populated for --as-of.
flags.name: --skip-pull

flags.help: Optional CSV subset of expected domains (recovery, running, sleep, stress, strength, nutrition). Default: all 6. Narrows the proposal-completeness gate: synthesis is blocked until every domain in this set has a proposal. Use this to say `I'm only planning for these N domains today` and unblock the gate without posting unused proposals. Synthesis still runs over every proposal present in proposal_log for (for_date, user_id), which may be a superset of this list if other domains were proposed.
flags.name: --domains

flags.help: Agent version string stamped on committed rows.
flags.name: --agent-version

flags.help: Keep prior canonical plan and write a fresh _v<N> id. Default is atomic replace.
flags.name: --supersede

flags.help: v0.1.13 W-FBC-2 (closure of F-B-04, option A default per reporting/docs/supersede_domain_coverage.md): operator belt-and-braces signal that every domain's proposal should have been freshly authored in this synthesis cycle. Synthesis appends a per-domain `<domain>_proposal_carryover_under_re_propose_all` token to any recommendation whose proposal envelope was authored outside the freshness window (default 60s) — the audit-chain signal that the operator's intent was observably not honored for that domain. The token surfaces in `hai today` rationale prose and `hai explain` recommendation rows. Also surfaces in the daily report JSON as `re_propose_all_requested: true`.
flags.name: --re-propose-all

flags.help: Skip review-event scheduling after synthesis.
flags.name: --skip-reviews

flags.help: v0.1.7 W21: emit a versioned next_actions[] manifest alongside the stage report. Each action carries a concrete command_argv, kind, reason_code, blocking, safe_to_retry, and after_success routing — so an agent can drive the full daily loop without consulting the intent-router skill prose. Schema is next_actions.v1.
flags.name: --auto

flags.help: v0.1.8 W43: with --auto, attach a per-stage explain block to the JSON output (pull / clean / snapshot / gaps / proposal_gate / synthesize). Reads already-computed data; never recomputes, mutates, or fabricates fields.
flags.name: --explain

OK.notes: v0.1.6: the proposal gate has three pre-synthesis statuses. `awaiting_proposals` = zero proposals; `incomplete` = some proposals but missing >=1 expected domain (synthesis blocked, hint names the missing domains); `complete` = every expected domain has a proposal (synthesis runs). On the first two, the agent must post the missing DomainProposal rows OR narrow `--domains` and rerun. `failed` is reserved for synthesis errors after the gate.
OK.shape: JSON report of the orchestration run.

commands.description: Remove an orphan / corrupt demo marker so the CLI can return to normal mode. Allowed even when the marker is invalid (the fail-closed escape hatch).
Close the active demo session. Removes the marker so subsequent CLI invocations route to real persistence. v0.1.11 W-Va leaves the scratch root in place; W-Vb adds archive-on-end behaviour.

Open a new demo session. Creates a scratch root at /tmp/hai_demo_<id>/ with state.db, health_agent_root/, and config/thresholds.toml. Writes a marker file at the demo-session location ($XDG_CACHE_HOME/hai/ or ~/.cache/hai/). Refuses with USER_INPUT if a session is already active.

flags.help: Persona slug. v0.1.12 W-Vb (partial closure): the packaged skeleton fixture for the slug loads from `health_agent_infra.demo.fixtures.<slug>` and the demo marker records a `fixture_application` entry, but proposal pre-population (so `hai daily` reaches synthesis) is deferred to v0.1.13 W-Vb. Use `--blank` for an explicitly-empty session.
flags.name: --persona

flags.help: Force an empty session (no persona). Default behaviour today.
flags.name: --blank

commands.description: Report runtime health: DB present, migrations up to date, per-source freshness, today's accepted counts.

flags.help: Override thresholds TOML path (default: platformdirs user_config_dir).
flags.name: --thresholds-path

flags.help: Override state DB path (default: $HAI_STATE_DB or platform default).
flags.name: --db-path

flags.help: Skills destination to inspect (default: ~/.claude/skills/).
flags.name: --skills-dest

flags.help: Which user's sync history + today counts to report. Default: u_local_1.
flags.name: --user-id

flags.help: Anchor date for freshness + today counts, ISO-8601. Default: today (UTC).
flags.name: --as-of

flags.help: Emit the structured report dict as JSON instead of the human-readable text view.
flags.name: --json

flags.help: Run live API probes against the configured credential surfaces (intervals.icu wellness fetch, etc.). Closes the diagnostic-trust gap Codex F-DEMO-01 surfaced — a present credential is not the same as an accepted credential. Default off so the cheap path stays cheap. Routes to a fixture stub (no network) when a demo session is active (W-X / Q-3 + W-Va integration).
flags.name: --deep

commands.description: Execute frozen deterministic eval scenarios for a domain (--domain) or the synthesis layer (--synthesis). Read-only — scores scenarios, never writes state. USER_INPUT when a scenario fails its rubric; INTERNAL if the runner itself crashes.

flags.help: Run domain-level (classify + policy) scenarios for this domain
flags.name: --domain

flags.help: Run synthesis-level (X-rule + run_synthesis) scenarios
flags.name: --synthesis

flags.help: Run a named scenario set (v0.1.14 W-AN). 'all' runs every domain + synthesis (judge_adversarial is fixture-only and skipped from 'all' until v0.2.2 W58J wires the judge harness). 'judge_adversarial' enumerates the v0.1.14 W-AI fixture corpus shape-only — no scoring.
flags.name: --scenario-set

flags.help: Emit machine-readable JSON instead of human-readable text
flags.name: --json

commands.description: Rank top exercise-taxonomy matches for a free-text query.

flags.help: Free-text exercise name to resolve
flags.name: --query

flags.help: Max hits to return (default 10)
flags.name: --limit

flags.help: Path to state DB (default: platformdirs user_data_dir)
flags.name: --db-path

commands.description: Reconstruct the full audit chain (planned / adapted / firings / performed) for a committed plan. Strictly read-only — never recomputes runtime state.

flags.help: Exact plan id to explain (e.g. 'plan_2026-04-17_u_local_1' or its '_v<N>' supersession variants). Mutually exclusive with --for-date / --user-id.
flags.name: --daily-plan-id

flags.help: Civil date of the canonical plan to explain, ISO-8601. Pair with --user-id. `--as-of` is the canonical alias; `--for-date` retained for backwards compatibility (deprecated, removed in v0.1.13).

flags.help: User whose canonical plan to explain. Pair with --for-date.

flags.help: Render the bundle as a dense plain-text operator report instead of JSON. For end-user prose, use `hai today`.

flags.help: Deprecated alias for --operator. Will be removed in a future release; update scripts to use --operator.
flags.name: --text

flags.help: Which plan in a supersede chain to explain when --for-date is used. 'latest' (default) resolves the canonical leaf. 'first' returns the chain head. 'all' emits the full chain as a JSON array (or sequential text blocks with --text). Incompatible with --daily-plan-id, which already pins a specific plan. See reporting/plans/v0_1_4/D1_re_author_semantics.md.

flags.help: State DB path (default: `$HAI_STATE_DB` or `~/.local/share/health_agent_infra/state.db`)

flags.help: How many days of historical context the first-pull adapter fetches (default: 1 → ~5 Garmin API calls). Larger windows give richer baselines but risk rate-limiting: each extra day adds ~5 API calls. Ignored without --with-first-pull.

flags.help: v0.1.11 W-W: derive gaps from the latest accepted state without fresh evidence. The session-start protocol fallback when `hai pull` is broken or skipped. Pass --allow-stale-snapshot to override. Output gaps carry `derived_from: state_snapshot` and `snapshot_read_at`.

flags.help: Fact origin (default: user_manual).
flags.name: --source

flags.help: Transport identity (default: hai_cli_direct).
flags.name: --ingest-actor

flags.help: State DB path (default: `$HAI_STATE_DB` or `~/.local/share/health_agent_infra/state.db`).
flags.name: --db-path

commands.description: Emit the canonical planned_session_type vocabulary (token + classifier_substring + description per entry). Source registry: core/intake/planned_session_vocabulary.py.
Validate a DomainProposal and append it to proposal_log. One of the three determinism boundaries the runtime enforces.

flags.help: Domain whose proposal is being written
flags.name: --domain

flags.help: Path to a JSON file matching DomainProposal shape
flags.name: --proposal-json

flags.help: Writeback root; <domain>_proposals.jsonl will be appended here. Default: $HAI_BASE_DIR or ~/.health_agent.
flags.name: --base-dir

flags.help: State DB path (default: `$HAI_STATE_DB` or `~/.local/share/health_agent_infra/state.db`)
flags.name: --db-path

flags.help: Revise an existing canonical proposal for (for_date, user_id, domain). Without --replace, a second propose for the same chain key exits USER_INPUT. With --replace, a new revision is inserted and the prior leaf's superseded_by_proposal_id is updated in a single transaction. If the new payload is byte-identical to the current leaf, --replace is a no-op. See reporting/plans/v0_1_4/D1_re_author_semantics.md.
flags.name: --replace

OK.notes: revision=1 on first-write; >1 after --replace. superseded_by_proposal_id is NULL on the canonical leaf.
OK.shape: JSON confirmation of the persisted proposal.

commands.description: Acquire evidence for a date and emit cleaned evidence JSON. Source resolution (v0.1.6): explicit `--source` > legacy `--live` (= garmin_live) > intervals.icu when credentials are configured > csv fixture fallback. Garmin live is best-effort (rate-limited); intervals.icu is the supported live source. Writes a sync_run_log row; does not touch the main state tables.

flags.help: Civil date, ISO-8601 (default today UTC). `--as-of` is the canonical alias; `--date` retained for backwards compatibility (deprecated, removed in v0.1.13).
flags.name: --date

flags.help: Path to a JSON file with manual readiness fields
flags.name: --manual-readiness-json

flags.help: Use a neutral manual readiness default (for offline runs)
flags.name: --use-default-manual-readiness

flags.help: Legacy flag: equivalent to --source garmin_live. Garmin Connect is rate-limited and unreliable for live scraping; prefer `--source intervals_icu` (the maintainer's declared supported live source as of v0.1.6).
flags.name: --live

flags.help: Evidence source. csv reads the committed fixture; garmin_live scrapes Garmin Connect (best-effort: rate-limited, unreliable); intervals_icu pulls from Intervals.icu's wellness API — stable and the supported live source today, but scoped to what that service exposes (HRV + RHR + sleep + load; no per-session running granularity yet). Default (v0.1.6): intervals_icu when credentials are configured, else csv.
flags.name: --source

flags.help: Trailing window size for resting_hr / hrv / training_load series (live pull only). Matches the CSV adapter default.
flags.name: --history-days

flags.help: State DB path for sync_run_log writes. Best-effort — if the DB is absent, the pull still runs but the sync row is skipped. Default: `$HAI_STATE_DB` or `~/.local/share/health_agent_infra/state.db`.
flags.name: --db-path

commands.description: Retrieve sources for one allowlisted research topic. Mirrors core.research.retrieve but exposes only the topic-token interface — the privacy-violation booleans are not configurable. Read-only; no network.

flags.help: Topic token (must be on the allowlist; see `hai research topics`).
flags.name: --topic

commands.description: List the allowlisted topics the bounded retrieval surface recognises. Read-only.

Restore state.db + JSONL audit logs from a `hai backup` tarball. Refuses if the bundle's schema_version does not match the installed wheel's head. Overwrites the destination — back up first if it has data.

flags.help: Path to a backup tarball produced by `hai backup`.
flags.name: --bundle

flags.help: State DB path (overwritten on restore).
flags.name: --db-path

flags.help: Writeback/intake root (JSONL logs overwritten on restore).
flags.name: --base-dir

commands.description: Record a review_outcome against a review_event. Carries the migration-010 enrichment columns (completed, intensity_delta, pre/post_energy, disagreed_firing_ids).

flags.help: Writeback root for review_outcomes.jsonl. Default: $HAI_BASE_DIR or ~/.health_agent.
flags.name: --base-dir

flags.help: State DB path (default: `$HAI_STATE_DB` or `~/.local/share/health_agent_infra/state.db`)
flags.name: --db-path

flags.help: Whether the user completed the recommended session.
flags.name: --completed

flags.help: Intensity the user applied relative to the recommendation. Ordinals used by summary aggregation: much_lighter=-2, lighter=-1, same=0, harder=1, much_harder=2.
flags.name: --intensity-delta

flags.help: Actual session duration in whole minutes.
flags.name: --duration-minutes

flags.help: Self-reported energy score before the session, 1–5.
flags.name: --pre-energy

flags.help: Self-reported energy score after the session, 1–5.
flags.name: --post-energy

flags.help: Comma-separated x_rule_firing.firing_id list the user marked as 'should not have fired'. Empty string records an explicit empty list (the question was asked, no disagreement).
flags.name: --disagreed-firings

OK.notes: When the target rec's plan has been superseded, recommendation_id is the leaf rec and re_linked_from_recommendation_id is the original. Refusal (leaf has no matching-domain rec) exits USER_INPUT with no write.
OK.shape: JSON confirmation of the persisted outcome.

commands.description: Persist a pending review_event for a recommendation (used to schedule the next-day review question).

flags.help: Writeback root for review_events.jsonl. Default: $HAI_BASE_DIR or ~/.health_agent.
flags.name: --base-dir

flags.help: State DB path (default: `$HAI_STATE_DB` or `~/.local/share/health_agent_infra/state.db`)
flags.name: --db-path

commands.description: Summarize review_outcome counts (followed / not-followed, per-domain tallies).

flags.help: Writeback root to read review_outcomes.jsonl from. Default: $HAI_BASE_DIR or ~/.health_agent.
flags.name: --base-dir

flags.help: Restrict counts to a single domain (e.g. 'recovery' or 'running'). Omitted = all domains.
flags.name: --domain

commands.description: Copy the packaged skills/ tree to ~/.claude/skills/ so Claude Code discovers them.

flags.help: Overwrite existing skill directories of the same name
flags.name: --force

commands.description: Create the local SQLite state DB and apply all pending migrations. Idempotent — safe to call repeatedly.

flags.help: Path to state.db (default: $HAI_STATE_DB or ~/.local/share/health_agent_infra/state.db)
flags.name: --db-path

commands.description: Apply any pending schema migrations to an already-initialized state DB.

flags.help: Path to state.db (default: $HAI_STATE_DB or ~/.local/share/health_agent_infra/state.db)
flags.name: --db-path

commands.description: Read a per-domain accepted-state row for a given date.

flags.help: One of: recovery, running, gym, nutrition, stress, notes, recommendations, reviews, goals
flags.name: --domain

flags.help: Inclusive civil-date lower bound, ISO-8601
flags.name: --since

flags.help: Inclusive civil-date upper bound (default: same as --since)
flags.name: --until

flags.help: Filter to a single user (default: no filter)
flags.name: --user-id

commands.description: Rebuild the accepted_*_state_daily tables from the raw evidence JSONL. Deterministic modulo projection timestamps — content/keys/links replay identically across runs, but projected_at / corrected_at columns reflect the wall-clock of the rebuild. Safe to re-run.

flags.help: Writeback/intake root. Default: $HAI_BASE_DIR or ~/.health_agent. Recognised audit files: recommendation_log.jsonl, review_events.jsonl, review_outcomes.jsonl, gym_sessions.jsonl, nutrition_intake.jsonl. Only the groups whose files are present get truncated and rebuilt.
flags.name: --base-dir

flags.help: State DB path (default: $HAI_STATE_DB or platform default)
flags.name: --db-path

flags.help: Allow reproject to truncate the projection tables even when the base-dir contains none of the expected JSONL audit logs. Refuses by default to guard against typo-driven data loss.
flags.name: --allow-empty-reproject

flags.help: Permit reproject to delete synthesis-side rows (planned_recommendation, daily_plan, x_rule_firing) when they would otherwise block the rebuild via FK constraints. Refuses by default because those tables are not JSONL-derived — they're computed by `hai synthesize`. If you pass this flag, re-run `hai synthesize` afterwards to repopulate them.
flags.name: --cascade-synthesis

commands.description: Emit the cross-domain state snapshot the synthesis / skills layer consumes for a (for_date, user_id) pair.

flags.help: Civil date to snapshot, ISO-8601
flags.name: --as-of

flags.help: User whose state to snapshot
flags.name: --user-id

flags.help: Days of history to include (default: 14)
flags.name: --lookback-days

flags.help: Optional path to a `hai clean` output JSON. When present, the recovery block is expanded to the full Phase 1 bundle shape (evidence + raw_summary + classified_state + policy_result). When absent, the recovery block keeps its v1.0 shape.
flags.name: --evidence-json

commands.description: Summarise sync_run_log (last pull per source) + runtime_event_log (recent commands, daily streak) from the user's local DB. With --outcomes, emits the code-owned review-outcome summary (W48) instead. No telemetry leaves the device.

flags.help: Override state DB path (default: $HAI_STATE_DB or platform default).
flags.name: --db-path

flags.help: Whose sync freshness to report. Default: u_local_1.
flags.name: --user-id

flags.help: Number of recent runtime_event_log rows to include (default: 7).
flags.name: --limit

flags.help: Emit the structured report dict as JSON instead of the human-readable text view.
flags.name: --json

flags.help: Mode: emit the code-owned review-outcome summary (W48) instead of the default sync/events report. Combine with --domain and --since to narrow the window.
flags.name: --outcomes

flags.help: Restrict --outcomes view to a single v1 domain. Omit to receive the per-domain breakdown plus aggregate roll-up.
flags.name: --domain

flags.help: Override the rolling window for --outcomes / --data-quality (days). Default: 7 for outcomes; for data-quality, the most recent N days are returned.
flags.name: --since

flags.help: Mode: emit the data-quality ledger view (W51). Combine with --domain and --since to narrow.
flags.name: --data-quality

flags.help: Mode: emit observed values, configured thresholds, resulting bands, coverage, missingness, and cold-start state for each v1 domain (W40).
flags.name: --baselines

flags.help: Mode: emit the daily-pipeline funnel — per-day overall_status, missing-domain frequency, and proposal-gate counts (W46).
flags.name: --funnel

commands.description: Run synthesis end-to-end inside one atomic SQLite transaction: daily_plan + x_rule_firings + planned_recommendation + recommendation_log. --supersede versions the plan instead of replacing it.

flags.help: Civil date to synthesize for, ISO-8601 YYYY-MM-DD
flags.name: --as-of

flags.help: User whose proposals to reconcile
flags.name: --user-id

flags.help: Optional JSON array of skill-authored draft recommendations. When present, overlays rationale + uncertainty + review_question onto the mechanical drafts. action / action_detail / confidence are runtime-owned after Phase A and cannot be changed by the skill.
flags.name: --drafts-json

flags.help: Keep the prior canonical plan and write a fresh suffixed plan id. Default is atomic replacement.
flags.name: --supersede

flags.help: Emit the synthesis bundle (snapshot + proposals + Phase A firings) as JSON and exit. Read-only — does not commit. This is the skill seam: the daily-plan-synthesis skill reads the bundle, composes a rationale overlay, and feeds it back via a second call with --drafts-json.
flags.name: --bundle-only

flags.help: Agent version string to record on every row (not part of the canonical plan idempotency key)
flags.name: --agent-version

flags.help: CSV-encoded expected-domain set (e.g. 'recovery,running'). When provided, synthesis refuses to commit unless every named domain has a canonical-leaf proposal in proposal_log. When omitted, defaults to the v1 six-domain set (recovery, running, sleep, stress, strength, nutrition). Pass --domains '' to bypass the gate entirely (matches pre-v0.1.9 permissive behavior — discouraged for agent calls).
flags.name: --domains

flags.help: State DB path (default: `$HAI_STATE_DB` or `~/.local/share/health_agent_infra/state.db`)
flags.name: --db-path

OK.notes: superseded_prior is NULL on canonical-replace commits and set to the prior leaf's daily_plan_id when --supersede was passed.

commands.description: Archive a W50 target row (status='archived'). Marked NOT agent-safe: archiving an active or proposed row IS user-state deactivation per AGENTS.md W57. Agents that proposed the row must NOT auto-archive it; only an explicit user invocation may run this command.

flags.help: Override state DB path.
flags.name: --db-path

flags.help: User scope (default: u_local_1).
flags.name: --user-id

flags.help: Target id to archive.
flags.name: --target-id

flags.help: Required for non-interactive callers (e.g. agents). Confirms the W57 user-gated mutation. Interactive stdin invocations are accepted without --confirm.
flags.name: --confirm

commands.description: Promote a proposed target row to active. Marked NOT agent-safe: agents that proposed the row must NOT auto-promote it; only an explicit user invocation may run this command.

flags.help: Override state DB path.
flags.name: --db-path

flags.help: User scope (default: u_local_1).
flags.name: --user-id

flags.help: Target id to promote (must currently be 'proposed').
flags.name: --target-id

flags.help: Required for non-interactive callers (e.g. agents). Confirms the W57 user-gated mutation. Interactive stdin invocations are accepted without --confirm.
flags.name: --confirm

commands.description: List target rows from the W50 ledger; default-active.

flags.help: Override state DB path.
flags.name: --db-path

flags.help: User scope (default: u_local_1).
flags.name: --user-id

flags.help: Civil date to test active-window membership against (default: today).
flags.name: --as-of

flags.help: Restrict to a single wellness domain.
flags.name: --domain

flags.help: Restrict to a single target_type.
flags.name: --target-type

flags.help: When --all is set, filter by status.
flags.name: --status

flags.help: Return every target row, not just active.
flags.name: --all

commands.description: Insert a wellness target into the W50 target ledger. Wellness support, not a medical prescription.

flags.help: Override state DB path.
flags.name: --db-path

flags.help: User scope (default: u_local_1).
flags.name: --user-id

flags.help: Wellness domain the target belongs to (e.g. nutrition, sleep, running).
flags.name: --domain

flags.help: One of the v1 target types.
flags.name: --target-type

flags.help: Scalar target value (number or string). Use --value-json for richer structures.
flags.name: --value

flags.help: JSON-encoded structured value (overrides --value when both are present).
flags.name: --value-json

flags.help: Unit string (e.g. 'ml', 'g', 'kcal', 'h').
flags.name: --unit

flags.help: Optional lower acceptable bound.
flags.name: --lower-bound

flags.help: Optional upper acceptable bound.
flags.name: --upper-bound

flags.help: ISO civil date the target becomes active.
flags.name: --effective-from

flags.help: ISO civil date the target stops being active (open-ended when omitted).
flags.name: --effective-to

flags.help: ISO civil date the target should be reviewed.
flags.name: --review-after

flags.help: Initial row status. Agent-proposed rows MUST land as 'proposed'.
flags.name: --status

flags.help: Why this target exists.
flags.name: --reason

flags.help: 'user_authored' (default) or 'agent_proposed'.
flags.name: --source

flags.help: What inserted the row (default: cli).
flags.name: --ingest-actor

commands.description: Render today's canonical plan in plain language — the non-agent-mediated user surface. Read-only.

flags.help: Civil date to read, ISO-8601. Default: today UTC.
flags.name: --as-of

flags.help: User whose plan to read (default: u_local_1).
flags.name: --user-id

flags.help: Output format. Defaults to 'markdown' on a TTY, 'plain' otherwise. 'json' emits the structured section shape.
flags.name: --format

flags.help: Narrow output to a single domain section.
flags.name: --domain

flags.help: State DB path (same semantics as other state commands).
flags.name: --db-path

flags.help: Print classified-state internals alongside the plan. Currently surfaces strength_status; v0.1.13+ may extend.
flags.name: --verbose

OK.notes: JSON format only emitted when --format json.
OK.shape: one of markdown|plain text (default) or a JSON object with top_matter + summary + sections[].
OK.verbose_surface: When --verbose is passed, a 'classified state' footer is prepended showing strength_status (one of the values in enum_surface.strength_status) and other internal classifier outputs as v0.1.13+ extends.
